findings.reasoning: Rotation logic in `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` (AdvanceModule:1754) correctly holds every freeze-relevant variable consistent. It re-requests only if the daily word is pending (`rngWordCurrent == 0`, :1779) or the mid-day flag `LR_MID_DAY` is set (:1769). The `rngLockedFlag` remains true, ensuring the lock is not stranded. Level increment and redemption window logic in `_finalizeRngRequest` (:1670) are correctly bypassed during rotation-triggered retries via the `isRetry` check (:1675), ensuring consistency. Slot 5 variables `totalFlipReversals` and `lastVrfProcessedTimestamp` are intentionally preserved to maintain purchased nudge value (:1785).

The request-to-word binding is robust across rotation. `dailyIdx` and `lootboxRngIndex` are never mutated during rotation, and fulfillment always binds to `lootboxRngIndex - 1` (AdvanceModule:1842) or `rngWordCurrent` (:1840) based on the preserved `rngLockedFlag`. A rotation mid-request strands the old callback via the `msg.sender` check (:1831) and restores the path with a new `vrfRequestId`. Even if a mid-day request is lost (when `LR_MID_DAY == 0`), the daily `rngGate` fallback `_finalizeLootboxRng` (:1299) ensures the index eventually receives entropy, preventing liveness bricking.

Validation in `rawFulfillRandomWords` (AdvanceModule:1827) correctly rejects callbacks from a stale coordinator via the `msg.sender` check (:1831) and stale requests via the `requestId != vrfRequestId` check (:1832). The silent return on mismatch is safe as subsequent retries are handled by `rngGate` (:1194) or `retryLootboxRng` (:1152). The daily/mid-day routing via `rngLockedFlag` remains correct after rotation as the flag state is independent of the coordinator address configuration in slots 31-33.

findings.title: Mid-day Rotation Re-roll Vulnerability
findings.trigger: 1. `requestLootboxRng` is called (mid-day VRF requested, `LR_MID_DAY`=1). 2. VRF word is delivered to `lootboxRngWordByIndex[N]` and `vrfRequestId` is cleared. 3. Admin calls `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` (AdvanceModule:1754) before the ticket drain completes. 4. Logic re-requests mid-day VRF (:1772) because `LR_MID_DAY` is still 1, overwriting the word in `lootboxRngWordByIndex` upon delivery.
findings.reasoning: The mid-day rotation branch in `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` (AdvanceModule:1768) lacks a check for whether the word has already been delivered (`lootboxRngWordByIndex[index] == 0`), unlike the daily branch. Since `LR_MID_DAY` is only cleared after the full ticket queue drain (AdvanceModule:525), a rotation performed between word delivery and drain completion will trigger an unnecessary second VRF request that overwrites the existing entropy, potentially changing outcomes mid-drain.
